I just went to court this week on a family case. I checked around for prices in the Lubbock area. Sara gave me a good price. Better than any other attorney. I’m a Disabled Combat Veteran, and was trying to get railroaded by my ex and her attorney. She was asking for more money and restrictions on visitation, with no legit reason. My ex was money hungry and lied on the stand. Sara was very aggressive and caught my ex lying several times on the stand!! Over all, my ex only got 60 dollar increase for child support, but I set more restrictions on her!! Plus, my ex wanted attorney fees. Obviously didn’t get that!! I HIGHLY recommend Sara Hudman, to my friends and family. All my family lives in Lubbock, which I live in South Texas.

My overall experience was horrible. Sara was first recommended by a longtime family friend that we trusted. He was retiring so he sent me to her. I should've known to look elsewhere from my first experience with her and her receptionist. She was then recommended by an acquaintance. I did my homework. Two people recommended her and she had 1 good Google review. Sara sold me on her being an advocate and fighter for my son. Someone who believed we were owed what we had not been given the last 15+ years. I bought her aggressiveness and thought she would do a great job representing us. She knew the lawyer we were up against, called him a bully and vowed she'd do a great job. After I sent my payment to her I heard from her a handful of times (via email only), never answering all of my questions and always having an excuse why she hadn't responded. I understood I was not her only case however if she can't handle her caseload than she shouldn't take on additional cases. I would have respected her more if she had referred me elsewhere rather than do a crappy job of corresponding and representing me. After leaving a message with her receptionist the day before going to court (because I had not heard from her AGAIN) I let her know I was planning on calling the courthouse and letting them know I had not heard from her and would need to reschedule. I wish I had fired her that day and asked for a continuance. She was not prepared. She knew what I had requested from the beginning and just wanted me to agree and settle with the foul mouthed attorney that was representing the Non-Custodial parent. I was embarrassed having her representation and the result was -- nothing. We had to testify and after 3 hours the judge ordered the same thing that hadn't worked since 2013 and 5 years before that. It was a complete joke. If you want to spend $500, have inconsistent communication with your attorney and horrible, unprepared representation, hire her. I do not recommend her at all.

reviews.text:
I emailed Mr. Martinez to attempt to set up some kind of consultation regarding a car ownership issue, but as his online submission form only had 3 options which did not exactly cover my issue, I selected the one that seemed to fit best and gave a summary of my issue. I advised him that I am at a point of desperation to get out from under the control of my abuser. I even stated in my email that if he felt he could not help me, I would really appreciate being pointed in the direction of someone who could. Mr. Martinez instead responded with a short, snippy, 1 sentence response to my email that did not cover any of my questions, to which I asked - since he cannot help me, again, please tell me who can. Mr. Martinez has yet to respond to my email. Based on some of his prior reviews, it seems that being curt and rude is just in his nature. If you are here to look for someone who is compassionate and eager to help his clients and potential clients, I strongly advise you to look elsewhere.

Edit: It's interesting to me that asking for a response to my request via email (which were both very polite) and then complaining about your terrible attitude is "being demanding". I know I have a terrible issue, why else would I consult a lawyer? Also very funny to me that you claim you planned to help as soon as I post about your nasty attitude. Have the day you deserve!

reviews.text:
My disabled brother was walking across his parking lot at work and was hit by a vehicle due to the driver's inattention. As it turned out, she was uninsured and he was not on the clock at work yet, so the company let us know they weren't responsible. It was a horrible situation and he ended up with multiple broken bones, surgeries, was hospitalized for weeks, rehabilitation, lost his job, and then, was unable to continue living in his second story walk-up apartment, where he had lived for 35 years. We desperately needed help. Loved ones did their part to support us and we are forever grateful to God for all who stepped up.

After a blood transfusion for my brother, the gravity of this situation became even more horrific. We couldn't know then all that we would face, so I called my neighbor seeking advice about what what we should do. My neighbor is an attorney and he recommended this amazing group.

They instantly called me back and came to the hospital to see how they could help. From our first encounter to the very last text message that I have sent, they have been a compassionate, attentive, and committed team, serving my brother like he was family, and doing ALL of this work probono!!!!! Even the Judge was moved by this!!

You don't often encounter people like Jordanna and Emma. Jordanna representated our family with grace, heart and true expertise. Emma consistently worked with me so that I could navigate this tremendous journey and help my brother find rest, comfort, and peace through a devastating & heartbreaking situation.

I was very hesitant to hire legal representation, but I can't say enough about how grateful I am that we have met these professionals. I thank God for them.

There is still good in every hard moment! God blessed us with people who care and I would highly recommend this incredible Firm!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Kristy and Gary, thank you for the kind words and trust in our law firm.  We were honored to represent Gary and wish him the best going forward.  We are a phone call away for anything you may need.

reviews.text:
Jodee Review

I've never been in a situation where I needed help in this manner. I was lucky enough to be good friends with Phillip Wildman, a defense attorney and owner of Wildman Lawfirm in Odessa,TX, and he advised me to reach out to Mrs. Jodee Rankin with the Fadduol, Cluff, Hardy, and Conaway law firm in Lubbock, TX.

Originally I wasn't going to do anything after my accident because of how I felt initially, but I am so glad that I did. Jodee Rankin and her team made the entire process extremely simple for me and my family, and they were absolutely transparent the entire time. From the her assistant, Emma Vasquez, to Jodee herself, and everyone between, I never felt worried about if anything was being missed, being done incorrectly, or not completed to the fullest.

They obtained necessary medical documents, bills that were accrued during my recovery, police reports and statements, and everything else required that I would've been absolutely oblivious to.

After all the dust settled and things were finalized, Jodee and her team made sure that I was not only taken care of after the fact, but also that I received the appropriate settlement for my family and me. I am very blessed to have been able to work with this team during and after my recovery.

I cannot stress enough how grateful I am to have been involved with Jodee Rankin and her team, despite the difficult circumstances. I now consider her and her team family, and I highly recommend their services to anyone who needs assistance after an unfortunate incident. Rest assured, you and your family will be in the hands of great people who excel at what they do.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jared, it was an honor to represent you! Thank you for the wonderful review and more importantly, for entrusting our team with your case. Being in a car accident can be a traumatic experience that can leave the victim feeling lost about next steps. We are so glad you reached out and allowed us to guide you through it. We truly enjoyed getting to know you throughout this process and likewise consider you family now. Clients like you make our job truly rewarding. From Jodee, Emma, and the whole team at Faddoul, Cluff, Hardy & Conaway, we wish you and your family the very best!

reviews.ownerResponse: We regret that this claimant’s review is considerably less than the truth.  We also regret that this claimant did not understand the disability process too well apparently.  One complaint sometimes heard from clients is that social security takes too long to review the claim.  We agree.  We did all we can do to move this case forward, but keep in mind that there are literally millions of pending claims.  Also, some claimants have a false expectation that they will receive benefits immediately.  In truth, it is common to receive two or three denials before we can win your benefits.  It seems that this claimant did not consider allowing us to stay on her case, it takes time to get through the backlog of cases that social security now has.

I said "How do you think I got your number? You gave it to me and told me to call you per your instructions". He went quiet for a moment and rudely said "Pay me $1,200 and I'll call you back".

Now in our original call, he may have meant to pay the retianter. However what he stated was only when something changed in my complaint that warrented his services. Either way, he provided his cellphone, not a office phone and went off at a "client".

We consulted several lawyers that told us it was impossible for a father to get a Standard Possession Order (SPO) for a child under the age of 3. Robert (Reid) McInvale made the impossible possible and proved them all wrong. Reid's possesses an expert knowledge in Child custody and he leveraged that knowledge in 2 court appearances to secure the father a temporary SPO and then later a permanent SPO. Prior to court Reid works with his client to ensure he has the information needed for a successful court appearance. Reid will not fill you with false hope and he will always tell you the truth, even if the truth hurts. Reid did a great job for our family and I highly recommend him to anyone seeking legal representation in a child custody matter. I would also like to give a shout-out Lawrence, Reid's legal assistant. Lawrence is always there to take phone calls and answered questions. He was a great help to us. Reid can make the impossible possible.

This guy is no BS. Even I was scared of him. I had a motion to modify a court order filed by my ex that wasn't close to fair or in my favor at all. After my initial consultation with Mr. McInvale I immediately knew he was the kind of lawyer that would fight like I needed him to. Once hired, we had a long meeting and went over everything that we did before (and what I did wrong in my previous agreement), and made a plan to right-side my court order as well as respond to the new one filed. Mr. McInvale and her lawyer spoke outside of the court room and then all of the sudden we were going to mediation. Mr. McInvale then instructed me on how to prepare. After that, we didn't even make it to court, my ex and her attorney settled and what I got was even better than anything the first order gave me. I feel blessed to have found him and will definitely use him in the future, should I need it. If you have a Child Custody/Parental Rights case I could not speak higher of Mr. McInvale.  If I could give him 10 stars I would!

Absolutely outstanding. As a father, I knew it was going to be a tough battle to win custody of my child. Reid is by far the most knowledgeable and consistent attorneys to work with. Don't waste your time and money searching and interviewing other lawyers. Mr. McInvale did my custody case in 2010 and I have always returned to him when I had an issue or a question. He is a very truth speaking and up front attorney. He is NOT like most lawyers in Houston who will candy coat things and make you believe you have a case if you don't. He is also NOT one of those money hungry lawyers who is inching for fame with the judge. He has a very good way of prepping you for trial, if your case goes to this, unlike other attorneys. He most definitely holds the 'Lawyers Creed' to its standards and one of the only trusted Attorney's I know. Not only is he intimidating (to the other side of course) and confident, but he is professional with his appearance and approach. In the long run I am extremely happy with the results I have gotten from this amazing Lawyer!

Mr. Robert Reid McInvale is the best lawyer I've spoken to, especially dealing with my very badly written divorce decree. I went to 2 lawyers before we found Mr. McInvale, they charged us about $300 for consultation and gave us no useful advice and basically said because of how my decree was written, they couldn't help me.
Then we found by goodness gracious and extensive research, Mr. McInvale. We called him the night before, left a message, and spoke with him the very next morning. It was the best consultation I had ever had!! Well worth twice more than what the first 2 lawyers charged me for a consultation! Mr. McInvale  told me what documents and proof I needed to get for my case. After gathering all the information, we went through the court process, he coached me through all of it, how to answer, what the opposing party might say or ask, as if we were in the court room infront of the Judge. He did not leave anything out and told me what all to expect.
The day before the hearing, my ex's lawyer (law firm rated well over 4 stars) made an offer we turned down. We then showed up to court the next day, Mr. McInvale and my ex's lawyer spoke,.. minutes later, my ex husband wanted to plea guilty!! He plead guilty, and was ordered to pay half my attorney fees!
Mr. McInvale is the best family lawyer you will ever find. He is honest, accurate, and very aggressive. You need Mr. McInvale, especially if you have a difficult case. Yes, he is very up front and he will tell you how it is. You also have to be able to handle his honesty and his aggressiveness. You want him as your lawyer! His retainers fee was more than the average lawyers, but he is far from average and is worth all of it, if not more!!! I highly recommend Mr. McInvale! We will continue to seek his legal representation in the future!
Robert was very professional and answered all my questions with knowledge of the law.   I couldn't have received a better free consultation.   Thank you

Just call someone else.
From the moment I was transferred to the "intake specialist" I had a feeling. The first question I was asked beyond my name was my address. I questioned as to why that was asked before anything about my possible case and was given kind of a BS answer (*put a pin in this, we'll come back to it). Immediately, the communication was difficult, I couldn't understand what this woman was saying; it seemed as if she was fading in and out. I asked about that, I was assured she was on "our office phone" and that she had no idea what would be causing it. She then seemed to catch a little bit of an attitude, I guess because I couldn't hear her. Having thought about this for a bit, the only thing I can think of is that she wasn't speaking into the phone. That she would began speaking and then bring the phone up to her face, that'd be why she seemed to fade in in the middle of a sentence. So, anyway, having interrupted me several times and NOT getting all the pertinent details, she then tells me she'll pass this to a lawyer and asks to put me on hold. Well, I'm also at work, so I asked if she could call me back. She said 'sure'... but, somehow, I knew right then I'm wasn't getting a call back. It's been a few hours now, and, quite frankly, I don't want that call back. Even if she did actually present that to a lawyer, I didn't get the chance to tell you that the 2nd had hired the 1st dentist and fired her after three months for the same doing the same things she did to my teeth. Missing what is a fairly pertinent detail, along with several others, I'm sure the reviewing lawyer would pass on it. However, and I can't put my finger on it, but I don't believe she actually showed anything to anyone. Now, back to the reason as to why the first thing you all asked was my address. I don't believe for a second that that is so that you can determine whether you can help me or not, which is what I was told. You could've just asked what city I live in and it would be the same. The reason for that is so that you can start bombarding me with junk mail. Unless, of course, you just won't represent people from a certain side of town... Hmm.
reviews.ownerResponse: I wanted to respond to your comments. I have my team ask a lot of questions, that is true. We ask these questions so we can do our job and do it well. There is no other purpose involved. Sometimes, telephone calls with today's technology just drop, the sound is bad, etc. and we've all experienced that. Most folks are happy to end the call and reconnect on a new call so we enjoy a clearer conversation. I'm not sure what the issue is but, we speak to folks all day, every day and generally have very happy clients who have an overall positive experience with us as reflected here in the reviews.

reviews.text:
I cannot express enough gratitude for the exceptional service provided by the Carlson Law Firm, particularly by Max. When my sister was involved in a car accident, it seemed like an endless battle with insurance companies. Despite a police report clearly indicating the other driver's fault, both our insurance and the at-fault driver's insurance were uncooperative.

Max and his team at the Carlson Law Firm took charge of the situation swiftly and efficiently. Within hours of contacting them, they had the paperwork sorted and had arranged for my sister to see a specialist. They ensured she received proper medical attention and even secured a rental car for us. They quickly got my sister's car out of the storage facility and into a repair shop.

Throughout the process, Max and his team were professional, empathetic, and dedicated to our case. They handled all communication with the insurance companies, sparing us from further stress. The results were outstanding - my sister's car was repaired, she received a substantial settlement, and we didn't have to pay anything out of pocket.

I wholeheartedly recommend the Carlson Law Firm to anyone in need of legal assistance after an accident. Their commitment to their clients and their exceptional service make them the best choice in such challenging times.
reviews.ownerResponse: Flor, thank you for your review! We are so pleased to know you and your sister received our signature customer service from our team, especially from Max. We strive to make everyone feel cared for through compassionate legal representation. Going through a personal injury claim can be a daunting process, one we try to guide each client through with ease, and work alongside every step of the way. Thank you again for choosing The Carlson Law Firm!

reviews.text: I cannot say enough how thankful I am for the level of service in excellence we received from Hayden Hatch with CTHG&E Law Firm.   I contacted them to represent me in a court appointed mediation meeting.  The attorneys were very kind, professional, and delivered a genuine level of integrity and willingness to assist me as I needed.  Attorney Hatch impressively equipped himself with the knowledge of our case in such a short period of time and represented me in such a way that it appeared he had been my attorney from the point this legal lawsuit began well over a year ago.  He demonstrated a high level of expertise, conducted himself very well and surpassed all my expectations given such a short notice. The professionalism and excellence in service is unmatched and I HIGHLY recommend this law firm in representing any of your legal matters.  They will not disappoint.  Needless to say, we walked out of mediation with a settlement we are highly satisfied with and Praise God for blessing us with good attorneys at CTHG&E Law Firm.  All Honor & Glory to our Lord Jesus Christ!  May all who reach out to this law firm be very blessed!
Highly recommend William Boyles, super knowledgeable. Very responsive.

I hired Hilary Hale to represent me in a civil lawsuit against me. I spoke with her on a Thursday (2/27) for our initial consultation she stated that she had a pressing case but that she would be in contact with me the following week (3/3). On 3/2 my previous attorney reached out to say that there was a hearing that took place without notice to my new legal council and as such I needed to talk with Hilary about this. At that time I reached out to opposing council and explained their error (lack of notification for one) and I'll admit that I wasn't civil about it. Then later that same week (again with no word from Hilary) another attorney emailed me a copy of legal brief and stated that he represented a 3rd party in the case. When I pressed him for more information he refused to answer any questions (my question was asking if he could explain the brief he filed in non legal terms) because I was represented by Ms. Hale. This confused me because I was paying for him to represent this 3rd party without my knowledge or consent.

When I finally reached out to Ms. Hale on 3/6 asking for the update I was promised on 3/3, she stated she was in a CLE class and that we would be in touch the following week. She also asked me to stop contacting both opposing attorneys (on 3/6) which I did.

The following week I again heard nothing until 3/10 when she emailed me a letter terminating our agreement and she cited my previous contact with opposing council and she stated that I continued to contact them after her asking me not to do so - which is false.

When I explained that I hadn't reached out to anyone since her email reply to me on 3/6, along with proof from my sent folder, I got no reply and now I'm forced to seek new and competent council.

So not only did she not keep her word about reviewing the case and getting back to me in a timely matter, she also went radio silent except to say she could no longer represent me despite me showing her I honored her request.

Personally, I would use caution before considering hiring them for other legal matters in the future.
Hands down best group of guys in the biz. No punches pulled no hidden agenda. Transparency from the moment you walk-in till the moment you win. If you been wronged, they’ll make it right.

Chris has gone above and beyond to speed up the process, even going out of his way to help me with Adjusters when they wanted to low ball on property (mind you this isn’t part of his job but he was kind enough to help).  The staff up front are always super helpful and go out of the way to communicate with all parties.

They have taken so much of the stress off my hands, seeing as dealing with insurance companies can be a nightmare. Highly recommend the Gorilla Law Firm... Best in town!
I was in a car wreck and wasn't sure where to start or where to turn. The insurance company kept giving me the runaround and didn't want to take care of any of my medical bills. I was referred to the Gorilla and they were able to walk me through the whole process (which made it easy and stress free) and got me taken care. Thank you to the Gorilla Law Firm!

My two sons were involved in a car accident a year ago and I didn’t know where to turn. Davis Smith and his staff showed nothing but professionalism and respect to me and my family and at the end they took care of us. I highly recommend them to anybody in the same situation.

I’m very disappointed with my experience at this law firm. My case began in 2023, and here we are in 2026 with little to no real progress. I initially paid $1,500 and was told letters would be sent out and things would move forward. After that, I heard nothing for months.

Eventually, someone called saying they were going to “review the case again,” but once again there was no follow-up for a long period of time. When I reached out myself, I was told my case had been reassigned and I needed to set up another meeting. Not long after, I received a $200 bill, which I paid. Months went by again with no communication.

When I finally called the office myself, I learned the attorney handling my case was no longer there and it had been transferred yet again. I explained the full history to the new person, only to then receive a request for a $5,000 retainer.

From my perspective, it feels like the case keeps getting passed from one person to another with little continuity, minimal updates, and repeated billing along the way. Communication has been inconsistent, turnover appears high, and progress has been unclear despite the amount of money paid. I would strongly encourage anyone considering this firm to ask very direct questions about communication practices, case ownership, and billing expectations before committing. At this time, I’m done with this law firm. I don’t feel like we’re getting anywhere other than I get bills to pay with little to no information and since 2023 all that that’s been done as a few letters have been sent to my knowledge.

I’m not sure that Google allows enough space to describe everything so I’ll just briefly summarize it.
I paid this firm $10,000 to represent me in a case that I should not be named a party in, and all they have managed to do is completely misrepresent me and fail to communicate discovery requests and answers in a timely manor. The office staff is completely rude and disrespectful and dishonest, and have made me look incompetent to the court and blame their lack of communication on “our office was closed for Christmas” back in 2024. And even to this day a second round of discovery requests were sent and due on December 5th and they did not notify me until today December 18th.
This firm has changed personnel countless times and fail to notify anyone, leaving clients in limbo and demanding more money. Please consider someone local and much more attentive to your case when considering a litigation attorney, not someone who doesn’t even operate locally

reviews.text:
Occasionally you have an experience with a company or service that you want to tell everyone about because it has such a positive impact on you. This is one of those times. I initially was considering a popular legal service online while researching forming an LLC. I was very close to committing nearly $1k for a chance that it would be done correctly, and I wouldn’t feel like I’d been swindled. While researching that company, I came across the Moster law firm, noticed he had a 5-star review, and began reading.

Those comments convinced me to make an appointment, and within a week I was sitting in an office, with what felt like hanging out with an old friend, a very knowledgeable old friend. He immediately allayed all my apprehensions about my idea for the new business, its legality, and the formation of an LLC. He explained the process thoroughly, but not dryly, and did not try to pressure or “sell me” anything. In just over 2 weeks, I have my new company, and I couldn’t be more thrilled.

As an added bonus, his services barely cost much more than the online service, and should any problems surface, I feel 100% confident he and his firm will have my back. That level of confidence in any business is rare these days, but it is warranted in this case. I absolutely cannot recommend him enough and if you are in the market for a reputable, trustworthy, considerate attorney, with the expertise to back it up, just save yourself the time and do what I did, hire him at the initial consultation. You won’t find better.

The Moster Law Firm earned 5/5 stars for their superior customer service!  Mr. Moster and his team were quick, efficient and helpful beyond measure.  Mr. Moster took the time to answer all of my questions in detail, and provided me with helpful instructions for the future of my business!  Working with The Moster Law Firm was a wonderful experience and I would highly recommend Mr. Moster and his colleagues to anyone in need of legal counsel.

Mr. Moster and his team are awesome!  We were made to feel like family and not just another client like most lawyers.  They are extremely professional and made my life much much easier when it came to opening my new business.  I would recommend to anyone needing business advice for a new or existing business.  Thank you Charles!

We are so grateful to have had  the opportunity to work with Mr. Moster. We had  several meetings and paid  several initial consultation fees to other attorneys that did not help us at all and made us feel even more lost than we initially were. Luckily we come across Mr. Moster. He did not charge us an initial consultation fee and was able to sit with us for over an hour explaining how he could help us with our business. We felt relieved and confident that we had the right attorney on our side. We  attained more information and knowledge in that hour consultation without even having to retain Mr. Moster than we had with all the attorneys that we had met before combined. We highly recommend The Moster Law Firm to any entrepreneur that is going into business or who wants to ensure their business is set up correctly.  Thank you Moster Law Firm for your help and for being quick, efficient and making our experience an unforgettable positive experience.
Thank you,
Nemo & Nallely

I learned many years ago that an educated attorney who is knowledgeable, and yet also truly cares about one’s clients, has the potential to become one of the more important bonds any of us will develop. For a plethora of reasons, many of them obvious – but one being simply a good attorney’s intention to lessen a client’s stress and remove a weight from his or her shoulders. Attorney Charles Moster, of the Moster Law Firm, fits that bill. Frankly, the acceptance of any attorney should never be a rushed decision, any more than one would stay with a family doctor with whom any member of the family is uncomfortable. It is, in the best cases, a professional relationship that can last a lifetime. I made use of another trusted attorney for decades. With circumstances changed and she no longer available, I, like many, did not look into a replacement until I needed one. (Probably a common mistake).
I actually made Charles Moster’s acquaintance through my daily duties as a journalist. It was later, when I wanted an attorney’s help with a legal issue that I felt comfortable in at least calling him to see what advice he might offer. It turned out to be one of my better decisions. The first thing he does is place a client at ease, in effect not rushing into X’s and O’s until he has tried to lessen or erase worries. I was thrilled with what struck me as personal attention by Mr. Moster, who first advised me well, and then helped me in an always efficient, even friendly manner. Everything proceeded smoothly, with Mr. Moster staying in touch through it all. In short, I trust Charles Moster. I recommend his work.

William Kerns
A-J Media Entertainment Editor (Lubbock Avalanche Journal)

reviews.text:
I had signed with a record label and the contract stated that they kept the master recording and in return, I would get (within 1 year’s time) heavy promotion and the cd released and distributed (digital and hard copy) to all media outlets.  They breached the contract. I did not get heavy promotion nor was the  hard copy of the cd ever released.  I contacted all of the radio stations and they all, but a few, advised me that the record label did not send them anything. The reason they had my music was because of me giving it to them.  I approached the label letting them know I wanted to be released from their contract and had asked for the master recording back.  They agreed to release me but they said the master was theirs and would not release it. In the contract there was also a loophole stating that I would have to pay X amount of dollars in return for the master recording if i back out from the contract for any reason.
I came to Kristen Beck about this matter.  She represented me and immediately sent them a letter.  We settled out of court and Kristen was able to get my master recording back for me.  She is extremely professional, knowledgeable, and informative.
Thank you so much Kristen!
God bless!

For those reading this review, it’s important to understand that in criminal defense the decision to accept or reject a plea offer is always the client’s decision. My role is to carefully review the evidence, explain the possible outcomes at trial, and give my professional recommendation based on experience.

Over the course of my career, I’ve tried roughly 140 jury trials, so trial work is certainly something I take seriously. Every case requires a strategy based on the facts, risks, and the client’s goals.

If you are someone I’ve represented and would like to talk about your experience, please feel free to contact my office. I’m always willing to listen and try to resolve concerns directly.

In late June 2021 my wife and I were looking to form an LLC in Texas. We wanted the filing and company formation to be handled in a professional manner. Our search led us to the Law Offices of Thomas Bush. Mr. Bush quickly made time in his schedule to accommodate a meeting with us to discuss our needs and set expectations. From the beginning, Mr Bush answered every question posed to him with a helpful, eager and professional attitude. At no time did I ever feel that Mr Bush was attempting to sell me his services, he was working to earn our business. He made us feel welcomed, informed, and confident that our fledgling company would be in good hands.

We hired his services, (very reasonable fee schedule) in the mid-morning on a Monday and by that afternoon all the required paperwork was already filed with the Texas Secretary of State. A week later, we had all the requirements met, the company officially formed and paperwork in our inbox.

As his client's, Mr. Bush made this large legal undertaking a quick and informative process. He kept us engaged every step of the way and also assures us that he stands ready to address any questions, concerns or further needs we might have. We feel his door is always open for us, and he is eager to assist.

In a time when customer service and professionalism are in short supply in the corporate world, the Law Offices of Thomas Bush stands as an glowing example of how business is done right!

I would recommend the Law Offices of Thomas Bush to any individuals or company needing the services of a professional and client focused business attorney!

-Jonathan Lindley
Co-founder and Chief Pilot

reviews.text:
I highly discourage this attorney.  What he charges is astronomical and he makes the client do the work.  In addition, he does not prepare client for court.  I went with my daughter-in-law before court and he was eating lunch and would not let us in and made us wait outside.  He finally let us in ten minutes before court and I asked him when he was going to prepare her and he just said she needed to answer the questions.  As many court hearings and depositions I have been to, I have never ever gone to one where your lawyer did not sit with you to prepare you for court.  Every dime spent on him was a waste of money.  He did NOTHING to help her get her children back.  He sucked her dry for money for 7 years and unfortunately, we went off of his reviews to retain him.  Do NOT use this lawyer.  These are facts that can be backed up.  I have to give one star because Google is forcing me.

Adding to my review. He must forget I flew to Texas to help her with her hearing. We did not arrive early. We arrived in time for the hearing. He did not prepare her. When she got on the stand, opposing counsel kept asking her to stop looking at Matthew for answers. That is because she was not prepared. Good lawyers prepare their clients for court hearings and depositions. He absolutely did not do this. He also was not prepared. He can say all the things he likes in the rebuttal, but I was there. I saw it. I lived it with them. Doing some work, sending documentation is not the issue. What she won, was done by her. My experience is not just with Matthew. There is a lot to compare to and he does not cut it. A guarantee was not expected from anyone.

If this honest review helps anyone, you are already ahead. Save your money. Talk is all you will get here.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I'm sorry to hear that you were dissatisfied with your daughter-in-law’s experience, although I must respectfully correct several inaccuracies in your review. We take great pride in advocating for our clients with diligence, preparation, and professionalism — values reflected in the many positive reviews we've received over the years.

In the time that I represented your daughter-in-law, I fought tooth and nail for her because I believed in her and knew in my heart that she was a good mother and the best parent for her children. Without complaint, I worked nights, weekends, holidays, and even while out of state on vacation fighting for her and her children. Her cases literally kept me awake at night because of how much I cared for her family.

After a SUCCESSFUL appeal in one of her matters, it’s baffling to hear the claim that I “did nothing” to help. Those who practice in this area understand how rare and difficult it is to reverse a trial court ruling on appeal — especially in family law.

Out of respect for confidentiality, I won’t go into the full scope of the work that was done or the legal reasons for our success. But rest assured, I gave everything I had to the representation and I'm proud of what we accomplished during my representation.

We do not ask clients to "do their own work," but rather encourage them to participate meaningfully in their own legal matters — a necessity in family law, where testimony and facts often come directly from the client. Additionally, asking clients to organize their sometimes thousands of pages of evidence into folders helps reduce their legal fees as it is obviously more expensive for me to have to do it. Legal representation is a team effort, especially in family law cases.

As for courtroom preparation, we meet with all of our clients prior to hearings to discuss the issues, expectations, and likely questions. Many years ago, my office only had 1 person sitting at the front desk, and our office was closed from 12-1 pm every day so that person could have lunch. Had I known before now (5 years later) that you were upset that you had to wait outside because you’d arrived early to a meeting, I would have apologized sooner. Obviously, due to confidentiality, I can’t reveal the frequency and duration of our prep time with your daughter-in-law prior to hearings, but she and I know that she was prepared as she did excellent every time!

Family law cases are complex, emotional, and often prolonged due to factors outside any attorney’s control. We do everything in our power to support our clients, but no attorney can guarantee a particular outcome, especially when the court’s focus is on the best interest of the child.

I cared, and continue to care, deeply about your daughter-in-law because she’s a wonderful mother who has built a terrific life in the face of overwhelming difficulties. That’s the reason I continued my representation through all of these years; and I believed in her as I still to today.

I wish that I could financially afford to continue to represent her without payment because of how much I adored her tenacity and kindness. She endured more than a mother should have to endure, yet she continued to remain strong in the face of such adversity. I admire her strength as many would not have continued to fight for as long as she has.

I respect your right to express your opinion, but I strongly disagree with your characterization of our services and our level of commitment to our clients.

Adding to my response. I recall you attending one of the MULTITUDE of hearings we attended. Not all of the preparation time I spend with clients is in-person in the 30 mins prior to the hearing. I spend hours on the phone in the days/weeks leading up to hearings to prepare clients. For that opposing counsel, it had nothing to do with her looking to me for answers as that is a common trial tactic he employs to rattle witnesses. Again, due to confidentiality, I can't fully respond.

I understand your frustration, but we have been trying to schedule an appointment with you for months to finish your case. You have an active case with us, which makes it impossible to help without contact from you.

To reduce costs, we only charge for the time that we actually spend on your case (rather than a larger flat fee). When you won't schedule appointments with us, then the only other method of communication is by email, which takes time. The best method for reducing your costs is to provide the information that we requested in the emails so we can finish your case.

Despite this review, we remain committed to helping you and your family, and we hope that we will hear from you soon to get everything settled favorably for you.

reviews.text:
A Trusted Partner in Our Family’s Journey
My wife and I had the privilege of working with Aaron at Carter & Rader Law Firm regarding the adoption of our son. From the very beginning, Aaron demonstrated genuine empathy while being honest and transparent about the process and the expectations we should have.
When it comes to something as important as our son's future and well-being, there was no room for error or misleading guidance. Aaron’s word, knowledge, and experience were evident in every detail—his attention to accuracy and execution gave us confidence during an incredibly emotional and stressful time.
Trust is never easy, especially when you're placing your child’s future in someone else's hands. But Aaron earned that trust—not just through the outcome in the courtroom, but through every step of the journey. Today, we wouldn’t have done it any other way.
Carter & Rader Attorneys at Law are now our family’s go-to legal team. From consultation to courtroom, they’ve proven that trust doesn’t have to be hard when it’s placed in the right hands.
Thank you, Aaron and your team, for who you are and what you do
reviews.ownerResponse: We are so glad we got to go on this journey with you and your family!  Thank you for the kind words!

reviews.text:
I had the privilege of working with Bonnie from Carter & Rader on my custody case, and I truly cannot say enough about the way she fought for me and my family. No matter how difficult things got for me—and even when challenges came her way—Bonnie never backed down. Instead, she pushed harder, stayed focused, and fought with a relentless determination I’ve never experienced before.

One of the things I valued most was her honesty. Bonnie always told me the truth—what it was and what it wasn’t—whether I wanted to hear it or not. That kind of integrity and clarity gave me trust and confidence throughout this emotional process. She was tenacious, strategic, and absolutely relentless in her advocacy.

If it wasn't for Bonnie’s dedication and the support of the Carter & Rader team, I don’t know where my children and I would be today. She gave me strength when I needed it most, and I will forever be grateful. If you’re looking for an attorney who will truly fight for you, Bonnie is a 10/10 in every way.

Thank you, Bonnie, and thank you to the entire Carter & Rader team!
reviews.ownerResponse: I am so glad Bonnie could help you out!  She is definitely a rockstar!

reviews.ownerResponse: Deborah, while I cannot comment directly on your son's case, I can state that the decision to testify or not to testify is solely made by the client.  No person can force a person to testify in a case, nor can an attorney prohibit someone from testifying that wants to.  The sole decision is left up to the client.  I think it unfortunate that you leave our firm a bad review based on decisions that are customarily made by the client, not the attorney.

reviews.text: I am sharing my experience with Mr. Lemond because I feel that I was lied to  and betrayed.  When I had my initial consultation with Mr. Lemond, he seemed very professional and courteous. He also warned me to be weary of attorneys in Lubbock that charged "extra" to go to trial. He stated that trials are about eight years out because of the backlog and that he himself did not charge extra to go to court.  Well, I decided to hire him.  After we had our first meeting, I had questions a few days later, leading to the ALR hearing. His assistant let him know I had questions and I was able to speak to him on the phone.  Immediately he sounded agitated that he had to speak to me on the phone and actually threatened to quit twice when I had questions, stating that I could just go find someone else if I didn't like how he was 'doing his job.' At that first meeting he said it was important to go to the ALR hearing because it was almost like 'discovery' and that it would be beneficial to my case.  Well, when it was time for the hearing, he then informed me that there was no use in going to the hearing because there was no way we could win.  He stated that my license would be suspended for six months and that his staff would help me get the occupational license as part of his fee. Now, when it comes time for the pretrial hearing, he informed me that for him to go to the pretrial hearing I would have to pay him an additional $2,900. Again, this is just to attend the hearing.  He went on to inform me that if we actually did go to trail, i.e., I did not accept a plea deal, I would have to pay him an additional $10,000 by the time the trial started, which was in about two months at the time. I felt compelled to accept the plea, even though I had not even seen the evidence because he would would not represent me without payment.  Now, come to find out, because he did not attend the ALR hearing my license was not suspended for six months as he informed me, but 12 months instead. All because he chose not to attend the hearing.  I am beyond disappointed and angry with Mr. Lemond and feel that others should know how he treated me to make a better decision when deciding to hire him.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to leave a review. I know people have real concerns and frustrations with the legal system, and I respect the right of clients to share their experiences. In fact, I encourage it, because reviews help people know what to expect when they call me.

That said, when what looks like a professional negative reviewer strings together inaccuracy after inaccuracy, it leaves me little room to respond while still protecting client privacy. “Dan” is using a fake name and has 93 hidden reviews (who hides 93 reviews?) which speaks for itself.

Here are the facts I can share. Early on I saw this was not a good fit and offered a full refund. I also provided free help with an occupational license so he would not be left without transportation. Claims of “thousands of dollars for a hearing” or “an extra $10,000 for trial” are simply untrue. Anyone who has worked with me knows I treat clients’ money like my own and avoid unnecessary costs.

I respect true anonymity and will always protect client privacy, even when I know who is behind the review. When you call, you get me personally, not a call center or a marketing company. And when someone misleads the public, you get me responding.

Here in Texas, we do things the right way. I put my name, my work, and my reputation out in public because this is how I provide for my family and serve my community. I will keep answering my phone, showing up in court, and fighting for people who trust me during the hardest times of their lives.

If you are looking for a criminal defense attorney in Lubbock who is honest, straightforward, and committed to doing right by you, The Fighter Attorney is here.

Following my mother's slip & fall at a hotel near Lubbock, I was not sure where to turn.  A colleague recommended we contact David Kerby, and immediately we knew we were in good hands.
Mr. Kerby advised us regarding the entire process, kept us well-informed throughout, responded to all of our calls and inquiries, and always treated us with dignity and respect.  David Kerby's  experience, professionalism, and practical approach were evident as he advocated on our behalf.  We will always be grateful that we had David Kerby and his team in our corner.

reviews.text: Camie was a delight to work with. Covid made things drawn out more than they would normally be, but I never felt "forgotten" or felt the need to call and check in on anything. reviews.text:
I reached out to this office to see if I could get some legal advice on a situation my husband and I were dealing with. First call was good. I gave them all my information and was told I would get a call back at the end of the next day. They told me they would let me know if it was a case they could handle and if they couldn’t, they would refer me to right people that could help. Never got a call back the next day. I reached back out a week later and was told they were very busy and they would call as soon as they had a chance to review our case. It’s been two months and still no word! The least you can do is give a courtesy call and be honest that you don’t care or have time for my case or at least recommend another office that can help! Horrible costumer service!

Reply to your response: I don’t know who you are referring to but that was certainly not us! We first contacted you in May of 2021 by email and again in September by phone. Our first contact was not November 12th and we never received a call on November 15th stating that you couldn’t take on our case. And we were never in touch with another lawyer out of town. What lawyer was that? Your response is a complete lie! Get your facts straight!
reviews.ownerResponse: We apologize our office was unable to take on the representation of you and your husband in this matter. Our records do show you first made contact with our office on November 12th and you spoke with a lawyer here on that same date. We also show you were notified on November 15th that we would not be able to take on your representation. Because it was relayed that you had been working with another lawyer from out of town, it was suggested that you visit with that lawyer to see if they had another referral. Unfortunately, we are not able to take on every potential legal matter that contacts us, but we do try our best to get back with callers as quickly as we can.

reviews.text: A very excellent meal, very reasonable prices, and very friendly and professional staff as always,,,,,

Excellent attorney. Handled my protective order and divorce trial. A good attorney knows the facts, doesn't get them mixed up during trial and knows where to focus and what to not give attention during trial. So it is hard to know if they will be good before you see them at trial.

That being said, they are made better when you are prepared, both with the facts and with what are fighting for. Be prepared to tell them what YOU want, as they are only advisors on the "how" which is unnecessary for you to know. They need to know the goal and all the facts.

This attorney handled my situation with precision, integrity and care. Thank you.

I had an outstanding experience working with Christina! From the very beginning, she was attentive and very thorough. She took the time to walk me through each step of the process and made sure I understood the answers to every question I had. I always felt supported and I knew without a doubt she truly had my back.

When it came time to advocate for myself and my son, she was tough, prepared, and absolutely on top of every detail. Her professionalism and dedication made a difficult situation much easier to navigate, and the positive outcome in my case speaks to the level of work she puts in for her clients.

Her office staff is amazing!  They were always kind, responsive, and willing to help whenever I reached out whether in person or on the phone. That level of care and communication made a big difference throughout the process.

I would absolutely recommend Christina Woods Duffy to anyone needing legal representation. She is an excellent attorney, and I’m very grateful for everything she did for me and my son.
Truly recommend Duffy Law Firm, I am thankful for Christina’s exceptional legal service.

Worst Law firm you will ever step your foot in
They definitely work for the other side
Literally forcing you to agree to things that are not in your favor specially if you don’t know much  regarding the legal system . Won’t not explain anything will only focus on charging you crazy amount of money

They lie to their clients to get what the other side wants. They work for the other side as well as to use scare tactics to get the other side’s interest. Communication is horrible. A Truly Amazing and Professional Attorney!

I cannot recommend Christina Woods Duffy and her firm highly enough. From the very first consultation, I was met with complete professionalism and politeness. Christina herself is an absolutely amazing attorney; she is not only incredibly knowledgeable but also genuinely understanding and compassionate, which made a very stressful situation so much easier to navigate. Her entire staff deserves praise as well. They willingly and patiently walked me through every single step of the process, ensuring I always understood what was happening and what to expect next. If you are looking for an attorney who will treat you with respect and handle your case with the utmost care, look no further. Truly a 5-star experience!
Amazing experience with this law firm!!!! Took charge and got things taken care of!!!

Everyone there was very professional and did exactly what I expected them to do.. great place and great people.. I hope I won't need them in the future but if I do I would definitely use them again and will recommend them to everyone I know.

I am not one to write to reviews, but my experience with Duffy Law Firm was beyond disappointing. The lack of professionalism, communication, and basic respect was shocking. I never once had a meeting start at the scheduled time — one was even over an hour late with no notice. I would reach out multiple times with no return call, leaving me completely out of the loop and confused throughout the entire process.

The one time I raised concerns or offered constructive feedback, Christina reacted emotionally and refused to accept anything that wasn’t praise. Instead of addressing or even acknowledging the issues, she became vindictive and tried to bully me into finding new counsel.

Despite receiving thousands from my case, she accomplished nearly nothing — and once she collected her check, she dropped me as a client without a second thought. The whole experience was beyond frustrating, and I wouldn’t recommend this firm to anyone seeking competent, respectful, or attentive representation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are sorry to hear that your experience with our firm was so very unpleasant. In reviewing your case, it appears that we differ greatly in our opinions related to professionalism, feedback, expectations, and results.

Often the public forgets that lawyers are professionals, very similar to medical doctors, who rarely, if ever, are castigated because the appointment does not start on time or if one deals primarily with their staff and paraprofessionals.

If this were an honest review, one that listed the attempts to resolve any issues, I would have little to say. As it is, I am bound by confidentiality and cannot openly discuss this matter to the level that it deserves.

I can say that I hope that your case is finalized to your satisfaction. It appears that you have a very serious personal vendetta against my firm, in that you have posted on three separate platforms. I sincerely hope that this animosity helps you find the peace you seek.

reviews.text:
If the only advice I can think to give this law firm is to "grow a pair," then you already know how terrible they are. Stop overpaying for Google and radio ads to "create buzz" and start defending your clients like you mean it. Or maybe go back to law school and sharpen up.
They take your money, then offer weak compromises after talking to the other attorney (always smarter than them) and doing whatever they suggest, then they tell you they "did the best they could." If you argue, they attempt to manipulate youinto thinking you deserve worse. File grievances, because they are an insult to the profession and need to be disbarred.

EDIT: Lol at their reply. They can't even defend themselves without sounding petulant and whiny. Definitely not a firm presenting respectable work. Make no mistake, this is how they talk to their paying clients.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We seem to be as confused as to how this review applies to us as you are about physiology and sexuality. Your name suggests that you desire or are jealous of women, yet you just suggested a firm of women “grow a pair” - an oft used euphemism for testicles. Perhaps we are not the ones that need to revisit our education.
Thank you for reaffirming that our ad dollars are working!

reviews.text: Lazy and incompetent.

Tina did an excellent job of taking care of my legal needs. I required some legal assistance in a fairly short time frame and she and her office staff did an outstanding job working me in and accommodating my timeframe. You couldn’t find a more professional pleasant office to take care of your legal needs!

I cannot recommend Christina highly enough. Her professionalism, strategic approach, and clear communication made all the difference in resolving my case. From the very beginning, she demonstrated an exceptional ability to navigate the complexities of my situation with both precision and tact.

What impressed me most was Christina’s ability to stay level-headed under pressure while keeping me informed at every step. She offered sound advice, listened carefully to my concerns, and ensured that my goals were at the forefront of every decision. Her strategic foresight and ability to anticipate the opposition’s moves were key in achieving a fair and favorable resolution.

Throughout the process, I felt empowered by her expertise and reassured by her unwavering commitment to my case. Christina not only protected what mattered most to me but also handled the situation with dignity and respect, helping me close this chapter of my life with confidence and peace of mind.

If you’re looking for an attorney who is both professional and compassionate, while also being a skilled strategist, Christina is the one to call. Her dedication and expertise turned a challenging situation into a manageable process, and for that, I am deeply grateful.

- Mike

My name is Deric. I was personally not impressed by this firm. They say they will get back to you within 48 hours and that rarely happened.  At one point I really needed to get in contact with Kaylee so I kept calling and calling and sending messages through the app for a few days with no response. When I finally got a hold of someone I was told she was on vacation. I just really wished someone would’ve let me know sooner. I know everyone deserves a vacation of course, but back when I was a manager at my job and had so many people that depended on me, I would always send out an email and change my voicemail so that it would say that I would be on vacation from this date to this date and to please contact a designated person while I’m gone. Also if you were to complain to them about anything, they would get angry or rude with you and talk down to you like you were an idiot and were always quick to let you know that you're always free to find another attorney after you’ve been paying them. I left a similar review previously on here while my case was still pending and Christina called me extremely upset and extremely rude and said she’s removing herself from my case and I need to find another attorney. Christina didn’t want my honest review of her law firm posted and pretty much forced me to delete the review.  Instead of Christina calling me and asking what her firm could do better for me she called and threatened me instead. This is after I had already paid them a couple thousand dollars and she knew I couldn’t afford another attorney. So I agreed to take the review down.  Christina and Kaylee are very condescending when they speak to you. I would not recommend this law firm to anyone for any reason.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Deric, sorry that you appeared so unhappy with our representation.

Good luck in all of your future legal endeavors.

reviews.text:
If you are reading the reviews, more than likely you are wanting to know how good they are. Christina was court appointed to represent me in my cps case so I did the same and looked at these same reviews and did my own research. My experience with Christina at first, I felt like I was just another case and she didn't have time to hear me. Of course my world was turning upside down as I was all over the place, and told her all the details even the ones that didn't seem to matter to anyone but me. And it may seem like that but I realized that Christina is a Beast at what she does which makes her busy. If you feel like she doesn't hear you , she does but it might just be best to write her an email with all those points you want made and I promise she will read it and pick out the ones that really matter not to just your case but you as well. She is like that honest aunt you know you can count on lol.

Ps. TO CHRISTINA ,
EVER SINCE I HAD TO MEMORIZE "INVICTUS" FOR 8TH GRADE ENGLISH, IT HAS BEEN MY #1 FAV. WE REALLY ARE THE MASTERS OF OUR FATE BUT WHEN I WAS UNSURE OF MINE, I WAS ABLE TO STAY ON COURSE WITH MY BOYS  BC OF YOU AND YOUR TEAM!!
WITH  THE UTMOST GRATITUDE,  THANK YOU ALL...
Sandra, J & M
reviews.ownerResponse: Sandra- thank you so much. We are the captains of our souls!

reviews.text: Duffy Law Firm helped me through the most difficult experience of my life with compassion and at a reasonable fee.  I am super grateful to Christina Duffy and the staff.

The staff at the Duffy Law Firm are personable and down to Earth. The attorney's care about the clients' needs and will work to meet them. Christina is great in the courtroom and will passionately work in her clients' best interest.
I would recommend them as well as use them in the future.

Kaylee long and this firm did absolutely nothing for my kids. My child kept calling her and  calling her because my attorney stated she was going to be my children's attorney that my daughter could contact her anytime which my attorney never told me I had to go through her to speak to  kaylee which was a lack of communication on my attorneys end in which was also a mistake to go with both attorneys didnt take my case serious. Although kaylee was negligent in her own firm, kaylee told my daughter that she would contact her, once she came back to Lubbock with her dad, my daughter called and called no response always got to marissa always was told ( shes not in the office, or shes in san antonio, ill take a msg) never a response. so I called and I called and was told marissa would take a msg when i finally decided this firm was a dead end and asked for a refund that i didnt want them anymore, I  got hung up on by marissa since she has no idea what she is doing, she's rude, completely unprofessional due to the fact of being the daughter of the owner of this firm, and when I called back I asked for the manager she claimed to be the manager if you're the manager and you're unprofessional, rude, have no idea how to even do a reception job why do I speak to you, so I hung up on her and she then got a msg to Kaylee right away after so many times to reach out,  finally so kaylee contacted my attorney stated i was being rude, from then on Kaylee stated I was alienating my kids from their dad which makes absolutely no sense because they were not with me I wasn't able to speak to my kids because their dad wouldn't let me through the custody battle. Also she stated my daughter was wanting to live with me just because I got a new nice house not because she didn't want to live with her dad not because she's feeling depressed living with him.  Do not hire this firm or attorney it's a mess and if you don't handle being mistreated by them they will take it out on you in court and side with the other party.
reviews.ownerResponse: This firm did not represent Ms. Hernandez. Ms. Long was appointed by the COURT as an AMICUS attorney for the children of the suit. Many times people do not understand the roles that appointed attorneys play in a lawsuit, nor do they understand that an appointment must be set to speak to the attorney. Ms. Long works diligently on all of her appointed cases. Further, as Ambriel Hernandez was represented by another firm, this office was forbidden by ethical rules to discuss the case with her outside of her attorney's approval. In addition, there is a court order that determined the fees Ms. Long would be paid and by whom.

Avoid Legal Aid – Especially If You’re a Single Mom!

I had a terrible experience with Legal Aid and attorney McDonald. From the very start, she never even got my name correct on official documents, which showed a complete lack of attention to detail. Communication was a nightmare—I could never speak to her directly. Every time I called, I had to go through the receptionist, who would relay messages to her, and then she would email me instead of just taking my calls.

During mediation, she told me not to bring up the domestic violence in my marriage because, according to her, “the judge doesn’t care.” That was beyond disappointing and completely unacceptable. Instead of fighting for me and my daughter, she pushed me into making a decision just to get things over with because she was too lazy to do her job. I felt completely unsupported during one of the most important moments of my life.

If you are a single mom going through a divorce, DO NOT choose Legal Aid. You deserve an attorney who actually cares and will fight for you, and unfortunately, that was not my experience at all.
reviews.ownerResponse: Family Legal Services (formerly the Legal Aid Society of Lubbock) does not have an attorney by that name. You may have us confused with a different organization.

It's like he doesn't listen when we told him about what happened the night that everything started, we have given him more than enough for him to file a motion to discover, and even a motion to dismiss. We will sit in court for almost 2 hours before he even acknowledges us, only to say reset. He has done nothing on any of the offers from the DA, nor made a counter offer. We've been dealing with this since April(first court date) it is now November and I not lying when I say my husband has had maybe 20 minutes of his time, but far from undivided attention. And we watch as more and more people are assigned to him. He is a personal injury attorney, not a criminal attorney. He doesn't care about his clients, their families, or trying to get justice. We watched multiple people come and go in court today, one which was a first degree felony max sentence life, assault of a peace officer and he walked on 7 years deferred adjudication probation, but when it comes down to it he does absolutely nothing to fight for his clients. He laughed in my face today, and asked me what he should do? Follow up with everything we told you, show some sort of initiative on trying to help. I literally hate this man, and feel sorry for anyone who has to have him represent him.

reviews.text:
On the 4th of April, upon updating registration, I was given a citation over a city ordinance which may seem minimal for most, but for me it meant it would infringe on my rights.
I first tried to voice my concern to city officials such as police chief who never responded, municipal Judge who would not address the ordinance and so forth.
So I searched online for attorney who specifically would know sex offender laws and I got to speak with the receptionist, who was courteous and helpful.
So I retain the service of Keith & Lorfing, and I was set up for a conference for the next day. I was asked about details of the Ordinance and my passed violation and later text additional cause numbers and charges.
By the 9th, five days later, my citation was up for dismissal and granted. By the 14th I received a call that the case was dismissed.
That was a relief, because of Attorney David Sloan, I knew my rights had been protected and that I would not be a victim from their impunity..... which would be an official not being held accountable for their actions, despite evidence of wrong doing.
Again, Mr Sloan was swift, ethical and always in constant communication on what the next step could lead to. I cannot extend my appreciation enough for keeping me away from being another statistic.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your powerful experience. We're honored David Sloan could protect your rights and bring relief so swiftly. Your trust in our firm truly means the world to us.

reviews.text:
June 4, 2020, I visited Mr. Hogan's office seeking his help in compelling the City of Plainview administrative & PD leadership to observe my liberty rights under the US Constitution &, more important, to put an end to what has literally been since at least Fall '13 an around-the-clock stalking of me. [i.e. Without any judicial authorization (Ret) Plainview PD Chiefs Ken Coughlin & Bill Mull & current Hale County Sheriff David Cochran have forced all of my devices onto a city-operated, county-owned cell-site simulator through which all of my communications & web traffic must pass before reaching my cell service provider's legitimate cellular towers & network making it possible - though certainly illegal - for Chief Coughlin, Plainview City Manager Jeff Snyder, Hale County Sheriff David Cochran, DA Wally Hatch, & others to read, edit, author, & delete my emails, social media posts, & complaints to & from the media, ACLU, police watchdog groups, & even to federal authorities at the Justice Department's Civil Rights Division, FBI,  DOJOIG, Office of the US Attorney, USPSOIG & others.]

Shockingly, rather than help me defend the rights of all Americans to be free from such senseless discriminatory harassment & victimization, Rob Hogan chose to become bedfellows with the City of Plainview while I was in his office! Allow me to explain:  While I waited to speak to Mr. Hogan I relayed examples of the discriminatory harassment & civil rights violations I suffer on an around-the-clock basis to a distracted young associate. Annoyed, I asked how much it would cost me for Mr. Hogan to file a simple 1983 lawsuit for the city's blatant, well-documented, & easily provable 84 month-long deprivation of my freedom of speech - specifically, Plainview City Manager Jeff Snyder's decision to recruit Plainview/Hale County CVB/Main St Director Melinda Brown to draft the city's draconian Social Media Policy as a pretext to block me from the City of Plainview official Twitter account, @PlainviewTX, from the latter part of 2018 to the present as retribution for my efforts to draw attention to Snyder's decision to ignore obvious 1st, 4th, & 14th Amendment violations, federal Color of Law violations, & specific instances of professional misconduct by officers including then-Chief Coughlin. I repeated my request for an estimate of the amount necessary to get Mr. Hogan working on the case. Hogan's young associate replied "Something like this would cost you a lot of money. We're talking thousands and thousands of dollars. Money you probably don't have to spend." (I had $12K.)

Even worse, while I was waiting to speak with Mr. Hogan & being insulted by the young associate, Mr. Hogan found time to take a phone call from City Mngr Jeff Snyder ending with his shameful decision to work for corruption and conspiracy rather than stand with me, the American People, Freedom, & the ideal of Justice for All. I can only thank God, that I was allowed to see first hand how little honor Mr. Hogan & his associates have. At least now, I know I'll never make the mistake of retaining the Hogan Firm which would be the equivalent of greasing the wheels of corruption.

UPDATE: 3 YEARS have passed since I wrote this very detailed review.  I am astonished that the Hogan Law Firm has made no effort to contact me neither to apologize to me nor to explain their shameful conduct.

I urge all Americans to remember what ought be every Americans most sacred pledge - the Pledge of Allegiance. The last few words are the most sacred. They serve to make clear to what exactly it is that we are pledging:

One Nation, under God, with LIBERTY & JUSTICE FOR ALL.

How shameful that Rob Hogan, Plainview City Manager Jeffrey Snyder & Hale County Judge David Mull have all failed - & continue to fail - to honor their Pledge.

Do your self a Solid by hiring this outstanding Attorney Kyra Blankenship. She is nothing short of extraordinary. Not only did she take on a shady insurance company, but she also battled the VA, government red tape, and all the endless delays designed to wear you down. Through it all, she never wavered—her strength, professionalism, and genuine care carried me through the hardest parts of the process. She came out on top, and she proved without question that she is a true winner, 1000% dedicated to her clients. I am forever grateful for her compassion and relentless fight for justice. In my opinion she is Hands Down one of the best, if not the best P.C. Attorney in Lubbock Texas.

I don’t even know where to begin because saying thank you to Kyra feels like not nearly enough. When I got into my accident, I was overwhelmed, injured, and had no idea where to turn. From the very first call, Kyra treated me like more than just another case file she treated me like a person. A human being who had just gone through something terrifying.

She didn’t just handle the legal side like a pro (which she absolutely did) she handled me with patience, honesty, and a calm kind of strength that I didn’t even realize I needed. She explained everything clearly, never rushed me, and always kept me updated. It felt like she took the weight off my shoulders and carried it for me, one step at a time.

Kyra fought hard for me. She made sure I got the medical care I needed, helped me understand what was fair in terms of a settlement, and wouldn’t back down when the insurance company tried to lowball me. I never felt like I was alone because I knew she had my back, every step of the way.

If you’re reading this and wondering if you should call her stop wondering. Just do it. Kyra is the kind of attorney you hope to find but rarely ever do. I’ll never forget what she did for me. I’ll always be grateful.

She didn’t just win my case she gave me peace, and that’s something I can never repay.

Thank you, Kyra.

reviews.text:
I am pleased and appreciative as I write this review for Reim Law Office. My interactions with her have been absolutely transformative. Confronting immigration charges, particularly complicated ones like mine, was quite scary. I was accused of fraudulent marriage, which was entirely false. The addition of a 204C to my file made the situation much more intimidating, especially because it was the result of insufficient paperwork at the time of my application.

From my first contact with Reim Law Office, I was greeted with comfort and a degree of professionalism that was well beyond my expectations. EJ, in particular, became a source of optimism and confidence at one of my most difficult times. When I first submitted my case to her, EJ looked me in the eye and said, "Although I have never handled a case precisely like yours, I want to reassure you that we will fight this one, and I am certain that we will triumph."

These remarks were more than just consoling; they were a pledge of unwavering support and devotion. EJ and the whole Reim Law Office staff went above and beyond, tackling each issue with competence, dedication, and an unwavering will to achieve justice. Their relentless efforts and smart approach changed what appeared to be an insurmountable challenge into a success I had only dreamed of.

Today, I am free of the charges that formerly hung over my life, and I credit this miraculous result solely to EJ's hard work and unshakable confidence. Her precise attention to detail, kindness, and unwavering pursuit of the truth have had a significant influence on my life. FINAL WORD: Case approved.

If you want legal assistance, particularly with complex immigration matters, look no further. Reim Law Office, and EJ in particular, provide not just legal skill but also a genuine, true devotion to their customers. I am immensely thankful for their help and the good conclusion of my case. I sincerely thank the entire staff at Reim Law Office!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much Ed for this wonderful review. I am happy we were able to help you get your approval after so many years and attempts of you trying to get this approved. We are happy that in choosing us, we were able to accomplish this for you.
Thank you for your review

reviews.text: Great firm with great people. Incredibly helpful and knowledgeable. I’d recommend them to anyone looking for legal assistance- big shoutout to Camila and Geoffrey! Thank you guys.

A Lifeline During the Toughest Time of My Life

I never imagined I would find myself navigating the justice system—it was one of the most overwhelming and frightening experiences of my life. But from the moment my family found Geoffrey and his team, I knew I was in the right hands. They not only guided me legally but also brought me emotional comfort and reassurance when I needed it most.

Geoffrey’s leadership and compassion gave me back a sense of hope when I thought my life was over. Kevin was exceptional in his support—steadfast, kind, and incredibly understanding. Catherine was quick, responsive, and thorough with mitigation efforts that made a huge difference in my case. And Gavvy—despite the endless questions I sent her way—never hesitated to help. She always responded with patience and clarity, making me feel heard and supported.

The transparency, professionalism, and grace this team showed were unmatched. Their guidance was a beacon of light during the darkest chapter of my life. I will forever be grateful to them—not just for their legal expertise, but for the way they treated me like a human being, not just a case.

Thank you, Geoffrey and team. You gave me my life back.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Heather Polk. We take any rating less than five stars very seriously, unfortunately, we have no record or recollection of any current or former clients with your name, nor can we verify anything about your identity, from your name, in our records.

We are very proud of our numerous five-star ratings/reviews across Google, Facebook, and Rate A Biz, and we work to quickly resolve any issues that may lead to a one-star rating. Thankfully, in the years since our establishment, we have only once had to address a one-star review.
