reviews.text:
Nice guy, but horrible attorney. We spoke on multiple occasions and he had to place me on hold, and even one time he had to hang up and call me back to answer my questions about my son's case. In the end, he didn't keep his word to "fight" for my son but instead convinced him to take a plea bargain. He constantly repeated that he didn't want to take the case before Judge Darnell - I thought that was what lawyers thrived on was "fighting" a case out in court and making the best case they could??? Instead of being an advocate for my son, his approach was scare tactics - "if we go before the judge you are probably going to get more time than the plea offer". I don't condone my son's actions but I realized pretty fast that the system is set up to incarcerate ($$$$) from the lawyers all the way to TDC. Good luck if you hire this guy. A court-appointed attorney could have gotten the same results!
_____________________________________________

I'll add to this post since there is not an option to comment on the owner's comment:
I totally expected everything you said in your comments is what you would say. I'll admit that was a pretty good defense against my position - that's all I asked for you to do for my son was to "provide a defense". I'll stand by my position that a plea bargain is not a "fight".

As for this being your first negative review, I'm sure after what I witnessed, there will be more to come. In any case, it is what it is at this point. My son will do his time, and I'll still be here for him when he gets out. I've advocated for him since he was born, and will always do so.

You know you made sure to mention "your son, my client" several times in your comments, and that's true. Different than your  position when I handed you the $$$$! That was one if the reasons we hired you is so that you could communicate to me about my ADULT son. My son signed off for you to be able to speak to me, and you did speak to me whenever I called you. You visited my son maybe 3 times from start to finish. When I paid you, you told you would go see him later that day or the next day - it took you 5 days to visit him for the first time. I knew then I had made a mistake in hiring you because you didn't keep your word. Learning experience for all. As for not having his file available for phone conversations is appaling you would say that - I never reached you when I called - you always had to return my call, which one would think you would have his file right in front of you??? If not, why return the call unprepared???

Hopefully, the feedback will make you a better attorney IF you recognize the truth in it and not just discard it like you did everything else. We didn't ask for magic - we just asked for a "fight" and for your "best shot" - and you said you gave it - we will have to agree to disagree. God bless you.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Mr. McAdoo,

I would first like to thank you for taking the time to offer a review of our law office. We have been in business for over four years, and this is our very first negative review, so we take such a review very seriously. I would like to address some of the comments in your review. Please know my response is limited by Google, but I will respond to the best of my ability.

1. "We spoke on multiple occasions and he had to place me on hold, and even one time he had to hang up and call me back to answer my questions about my son's case."

You are absolutely correct. We did speak on multiple occasions and on some of those occasions I did have to place you on hold, or even ask for the opportunity to return your call at a later time. Although a lawyer does not have an obligation to confer with family members of an adult client, even if the lawyer has been granted permission to do so, I make every effort to keep the family members of my clients informed, if the client has requested I do so. Many times, as in your situation, when a family member calls, I do not have the client's file readily available. Rather than asking my assistant to leave the family member on hold, I immediately take the call, if I am available, and then retrieve the client file or find the answer to the question posed by the family member. There are sometimes circumstances in which discovering the answer to a family member's questions can take more time than I believe is reasonable to ask a person to remain on the line. In those circumstances, as you learned, I will ask the family member for the opportunity to conduct the necessary research and return the family member's call.

2. " . . . he didn't keep his word to "fight" for my son but instead convinced him to take a plea bargain."

I am sorry that you do not believe that I "fought" for your son. I became a lawyer because I am passionate about advocating for those that are unable to advocate for themselves. You may be familiar with the title "Attorney and Counselor at Law". Part of being a counselor at law is the disclosure of pertinent information that may aid a client in making a decision regarding the manner in which the client wishes to proceed in a given legal matter. I cannot address specifically what your son and I discussed because that information is confidential, and I take my client's expectation of confidentiality very seriously. I can say that, in criminal matters wherein a client seeks to accept a plea offer, I always advise my clients of their right to a trial or hearing (depending on the matter), the collateral consequences of their plea, the range of punishment for the offense charged, and I go so far as to have them sign documentation signifying that they have been advised of all the above.

3. "He constantly repeated that he didn't want to take the case before Judge Darnell . . ."

Again, I cannot speak about what was specifically stated to your son, but I can say that I have had hearings and trials of varying types before the Honorable Judge Darnell of the 140th District Court.

4. "Instead of being an advocate for my son, his approach was scare tactics . . ."

I'm sincerely sorry if you or your son perceived my disclosure of all relevant information as a "scare tactic". As an advocate and a fiduciary, it is my responsibility to ensure that my client has all the information necessary to make an informed decision regarding their legal matter. The client is the ultimate decision maker, in this case, regarding whether to accept a plea offer or proceed to a hearing. I am responsible for facilitating the client's ability to make that decision and that often times means the disclosure of information that may have an effect on the client's ultimate decision. Nonetheless, my sole intent was to provide my client, your son, the information necessary to make a decision; it was never my intent to use "scare tactics".

Again Mr. McAdoo, we appreciate your feedback. Thank you sir.

reviews.text:
Excellent professional attorney Anthony with horrible and disrespect manager Ms Maria Oviedo as shown in the figure.

Firm known for immigration expertise in Texas.
I arrived early at 11:25 AM for my 11:30 AM consultation with Attorney Anthony Skeens, who provided an excellent, professional session around 12:30 PM, offering valuable advice on my immigration matters. However, I endured nearly an hour of waiting due to scheduling delays (nobody explained). Politely inquiring at the front desk mid-wait (as captured on CCTV), I was assured the attorney was nearly finished.
Frustrated but courteous, I checked again after an additional seven minutes, only for the manager to approach aggressively. She insisted on issuing a refund and demanded I leave immediately, without processing it or allowing my consultation—which Attorney Skeens ultimately handled professionally. This disrespectful treatment undermines your firm’s reputation; no client, especially one seeking immigration support, should face such hostility from staff when addressing legitimate delays.

Hope Mr. David Strange will notice this.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you, Mr. Guo.
I think the nature of taking pictures of someone, although in a public space, may speak more about you than about our customer service.

We appreciate you sharing your experience, and we remind you that customer service staff is not at fault for delays and is to be treated with respect, at our office and elsewhere. And ...... Mr. Anthony Skeen is definitely worth the wait, as you soon found out! ;)

Lastly, our policy of stepping up for our staff comes directly from Mr. Strange, who leads by example and has always told us we should not tolerate disrespect. I did not tell you to go elsewhere; I offered you a refund of your consultation fee if you preferred to go elsewhere after you became quite agitated. Nevertheless, your point about the delay in your consultation is well taken, and we will be certain to address the issue.

- Maria Oviedo

reviews.text:
Good morning. Here's my story: I was looking for lawyers to file my residency papers, and before going to the Whittenbur lawyers in Lubbock, TX, I had consultations with Maria Oviedo, the lawyer, and her secretary, Anavel Mendoza. I looked for lawyers for my case, but no one could give me the answer I needed. My request was to get my residency sorted out within the United States without leaving the country, and they all told me to go to Mexico and sort it out at the consulate. They told me to go there and have the appointment at the consulate. I think many lawyers don't want to fight the cases; they just file the papers and tell you, "We'll file your papers, you go to Mexico, go to the immigration appointment, and that's it." They said, "If you get it sorted, you get it sorted, and if not, we'll see what happens." One lawyer quoted me $12,000. Another lawyer wouldn't give me a price for my case; they would file the papers and then tell me what to do. I think that's unprofessional. Both of those lawyers are from Amarillo, TX, and they didn't even guarantee anything.

A relative told me about the Whittenbur lawyers in Lubbock, TX, specifically attorney Maria Oviedo and her secretary Anavel Mendoza. They told me they would handle my case to get my immigration status resolved here in the United States. From the beginning, they told me the price, the plan, and the requirements. I believe God put them in my path; it was exactly what I needed to do—fix things here. They were very professional. If immigration didn't respond within a certain timeframe, they would contact immigration and then call me to let me know what was happening with my paperwork and permit. They were very attentive to my case. They were constantly calling or texting me about my case, very attentive, and provided excellent guidance. Especially during the interview with immigration. They told me to attend an orientation to prepare for the interview. The attorney participated in the interview via cell phone. She even intervened once during the interview because the immigration agent wouldn't let her answer. She reiterated the key points of the interview, which was the final step. And the last time I entered the country, the agent wouldn't let me answer. Thank God we passed the interview and now we wait. After a week, my residency arrived after 30 years in the USA, and my case took two years.
The residency arrived!
THANK GOD!
A THOUSAND THANKS TO ATTORNEY MARIA OVIEDO AND HER TEAM!
A THOUSAND THANKS TO ANAVEL MENDOSA!
GOD BLESS THEM!
I invite everyone to come see attorney Maria Oviedo and Anavel Mendoza.
They are young and very professional!
Words cannot express my gratitude. If I can help in any way, I am at your service with my experience from the immigration interview.
Thank you for anything.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your kind words, Miguel! It's wonderful to see happy endings like yours. Enjoy your new status, and we'll see you here soon for your citizenship application! Best wishes. 💗

I was calling Law Firms to get Consultation and Retainer Fee Prices to hire an attorney for a nasty TRO and Child Custody Case, I’m currently going through and came across this Law Firm. He gave me advice on my Child Custody Case as a whole, on my temporary agreements, how I could fight it, what will look good in court, what I should expect the opposing party’s attorney to file next, and even had all the important questions to ask on details that will matter when we go to court. He actually pays attention and cares!!! Even when I’ve paid for consultations and hired attorneys in the past for Child Custody I never got someone who cared like this and give me so much advice even after years of working with them. reviews.text:
Edit: this was a five star review but knowing what I do now, I can’t not say something.
I was arrested in 2022 for possession charges. I told Mr. Payne a brief overview of what happened and he accepted my case after a $5k retainer fee. I got another bill for $5k a few weeks after. I didn’t hear anything from them for close to a year but figured no news is good news. Finally I get a call explaining that he struck a deal with DA and made it seem like I was lucky to not be going to prison for a few years and he was the reason for it. In lieu of prison time I would be on a year probation plus community service. Seven months into my year, it was time to go on harvest and informed my P.O that I would not be there that month and I thought we were in agreement which was not the case. I got another warrant for my arrest and was informed by Tray Payne that they were dropping me as a client. I was contacted by the court appointed attorney who said she would be taking care of everything and within one month got a call back from her saying that she got all charges dismissed for lack of evidence and the mishandling of evidence and that the officers were fired not long after my arrest for the way they handled my case with prejudice. Which is what I tried to tell Tray in the first place, but I don’t think he believed me. I think he was just able to strike a deal without even looking at any of the evidence or lack there of.. so now I have felonies on my record and have trouble finding jobs because of his complacency and lack of care for his clients wellbeing.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your review and the initial five-star rating.
We thoroughly reviewed the evidence in your case and advocated based on the facts available, leading to the initial incredibly positive outcome and 5 star review. Any suggestion of complacency or lack of care is unfounded. As Attorneys, we are not responsible for each choice that a client makes. However, we often do get blamed if the choices don't turn out well. We are also not allowed to comment or violate attorney/client privilege. If we were allowed to comment on that statements above, it would show the truth of the matter asserted is false. If you'd like to discuss this privately, please contact me directly at 806-368-8712.
Thank you, Maziar!

reviews.text:
Testimonial for Matt Chandler and PPTC Law Firm
We would like to express our sincere gratitude to Matt Chandler for his exceptional legal guidance during an extremely challenging family situation. When we unexpectedly became guardians for our great-grandchildren who had experienced a traumatic situation, we found ourselves navigating unfamiliar legal territory.
Matt met with us in April 2023 and immediately provided the professional support and compassionate understanding we needed. His expertise in family law was invaluable as he helped us navigate the complex legal processes spanning across New Mexico and Texas jurisdictions.
Throughout our nearly two-year journey involving multiple court appearances, hearings, and virtual meetings, Matt remained encouraging and professional. The entire staff at PPTC Law Firm was consistently helpful and attentive to our needs. Our case ultimately reached a positive resolution with the assistance of Lori Pruitt.
We were so impressed with Matt's professionalism and dedication that we have also engaged him to handle business matters related to the reorganization of corporate ownership as retirement approaches. His expertise across different legal disciplines has given us complete confidence in him and the entire PPTC Law firm.
For anyone facing complex family law challenges or business matters, we highly recommend Matt Chandler and everyone at PPTC Law Firm. They provide not only expert legal representation but also genuine guidance every step of the way.

reviews.text: Had some work related issues and spoke with Rafe on the phone several times.  I told him everything that was going on and he had advised me what to do to have a stronger case then what I already had.  I did exactly what he said and ended up losing my job over it.  Went to see him in person and took all documentation and recordings that I had and he was looking at some of the paperwork and his response was "well this worries me and this worries me" on a couple different documents.  Then questioned me has to how I came to the conclusion of discrimination and harassment.  I had already told him on the phone everything and him saying I had a good case and then after losing my job he questioned everything and acted like I didn't have a good case.  I would NOT take any advise from him and would NOT recommend him at all in any case.  By the time I left his office I was questioning the fact if he even knew what he was talking about to begin with and the fact I had wasted so much time.

I reached out to them because I am facing clear retaliation and discrimination after disclosing my disability and filing an EEOC complaint. I provided extensive documentation, including emails, policy inconsistencies, falsified employee record in HR reviews, and direct evidence of selective enforcement—the kind of evidence any competent employment attorney should recognize as a strong case.

Despite this, I was dismissed without explanation. When I followed up for feedback, I received no real answer as to why my case wasn’t worth pursuing, even though similar cases with less evidence get representation. It’s frustrating when law firms claim to fight for employee rights but only seem interested in “easy wins” or quick settlements.

If you’re looking for an attorney who will actually stand up for workers who have been wrongfully terminated, retaliated against, or discriminated against, I strongly suggest looking elsewhere.

This is Larry and I have used Rafe twice and they were both the best experiences that you could ask for. He’s very professional, and knows what he’s talking about. He knows the law extremely well and he has won two legal cases for me one of which had a DA that just would not quit, but Rafe beat him anyway. And put him up there with any lawyer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind words! We are honored to have earned your trust.

reviews.text: I have a greedy self serving sister.  My father who was 98 was taken out of his home and put into a temporary apartment. He fell and passed away shortly after he arrived in Lubbock.  My parents had a trust that was in place. My sister embezzled the proceeds of their house and bank accounts. Thanks to Field, Manning, Stone, Hawthorne & Aycock we reached a substantial settlement. J. Paul is a tenacious attorney which I would recommend to anyone who is seeking a litigator.

I called yesterday to request a consultation & spoke to a lovely young woman who assured me they'd get back to me soon. Called this morning to follow up & got "the regular scheduler," who proceeded to brush me off by saying, and I quote, "well you mentioned WPS (women's protective services) and he doesn't have time for that." I held my tongue & called back this afternoon to ask if Mr. Ware would maybe bother speaking to me since they said the quiet part out loud like that, or if I should just put in the bar complaint about discriminating against abuse victims, and this time the lady started babbling (incorrectly) about it not being the right area of law, so she scoffed and said I couldn't because he didn't represent me. You can't refuse service based on that.

Racquel Ware, formerly Blaylock, rented me a house in college a decade ago, and then she stopped taking my phone calls when I asked for my security deposit back after moving out. Coldblooded. Would not do business with her again.

Travis helped me with a child custody case. He was very attentive, helpful and calming. He understood the severity of my situation and talked me through everything so I understood exactly what was going on. I will recommend him to anyone dealing with a child custody case and will always be grateful for what he’s done for me and my child
Travis was a tremendous help during my divorce and child custody matters. He guided me through mediation and got me the outcome I wanted without ever having to go to court. Highly recommend!

reviews.text: Well I paid 10,000 and I truly felt he didn’t do anything for my son! He always made excuses he wasn’t able to reach him to talk about the case; he’s a lawyer! Every time I called the office he wasn’t available and I would leave messages which would never be answered. So the system is corrupted and I feel I threw away 10,000 in the trash. All I asked was a reduced sentence nothing more. I’ve seen others have a worse background and receive less or not have a paid lawyer (court appointed) and receive less or the same as my son. I do believe everyone should pay the price of their actions including my son; I just wanted a reduced sentence for him. Heartbroken mom!

I paid a $7,500 retainer, and talked to Mr. Ware a total of two times on the phone. My divorce was final January 7th, and I have contacted the office staff multiple times to see if I am owed any excess retainer funds. I was told it would be 7-10 business days. My first request for the excess retainer funds was in February, the second was in April, and the third was in May. It is now mid June. I was also emailed a waiver of arraignment for a criminal case for someone else back in January.

***UPDATE***
It is now August 28th, and I still have not received my excess retainer funds though I've called many more times. This place is a joke.

Called again 9/2. I finally received an invoice, and I got charged for sending emails asking for an update on my excess retainer funds. This is the reason attorneys have such bad reputations.
Travis and team were a great help in getting me a more favorable outcome. They were very organized and responsive, directing me through each step of the process.
Garbage attorneys with HORRIBLE attitudes. You have been warned! Go anywhere else!

Travis Ware’s legal services helped me tremendously. I was in continuous communication with him and Gail, they all stayed on top of everything to ensure everything was done correctly. I would highly recommend him to anyone in need of legal assistance.

I’m impressed with this law firm, they are all so selfless and really care. I was HARD to work with in the beginning, I was so worried and constantly bothered them but they always were reassuring! They truly treat you like family and will do their best to get your case won! Ms. Gail is so kind hearted, and Travis and Raquel are great!

Travis Ware helped me WIN a case that had been built against me. He was professional honest kind and confident. His staff was helpful and always offering helpful words and kind. This case was messy and I am so thankful he quit referred to me
Highly recommend.  I am so happy with the entire office. Very friendly and professional.

Travis Ware’s legal services helped me tremendously, I could not have done it without him. Professional,  Knowledgeable, dependable, and kind. I was in continuous communication with him and his staff, they all stayed on top of everything to ensure everything was done correctly, and in a timely manner. I would highly recommend him to anyone in need of legal assistance.

I cannot express enough gratitude for the exceptional legal representation I received from Travis and Racquel Ware during my custody battle. From our first consultation, it was clear that they genuinely cared about my case and my child's well-being. They were not only incredibly knowledgeable about family law but also compassionate and understanding of the emotional turmoil I was experiencing.

Throughout the process, Travis and Racquel demonstrated remarkable attention to detail and a fierce dedication to advocating for my rights as a parent. Travis and Racquel were always available to answer my questions, provide guidance, and reassure me during this challenging time. They prepared thoroughly for each court appearance, presenting a compelling case that highlighted what was in my child's best interest.

Thanks to Travis’s unwavering commitment and strategic approach, I achieved a favorable jury outcome that allows me to provide a stable, safe,  and loving environment for my child. I highly recommend Travis Ware to anyone facing a custody dispute. Their expertise and passion for family law make them an invaluable ally in the pursuit of justice for families.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Eric!  We appreciate you!

reviews.text: I cannot recommend them enough. They have a reputation for excellence and it is for a good reason, despite the clear cut few  examples of people with sour grapes that do their usual slander. You can expect honesty, professionalism, and tenacity from them. They are well worth it.

I cannot express enough gratitude for the dedication and commitment of Travis Ware. He took on an incredibly difficult child protection case that had been tied up in the court system for years—a case many would have turned away. But with his prosecutorial experience and unwavering belief in fighting for the safety of children, he stepped in and fought for what was right. Others may have given up, but Travis was relentless, ensuring a child was safe from their perpetrator.

For any parent battling a system that so often seems to favor those who do harm, please know that there is hope. Travis Ware not only understood the stakes but fought with everything he had for a child in danger. If you are looking for someone who will go to the ends of the earth for you, I wholeheartedly recommend him!
Loved Travis and Raquelle. One of the best in west Texas!!

This law firm is a joke! The office staff is rude and disrespectful! My son had to defend himself before he even went to trial for domestic abuse that he was never convicted of,  charges were dropped because there were  2 witnesses that were NEVER interviewed by the police department till after he went to jail and bonded out and paid 6 months probation THEN Travis had those 2 witnesses give statements to the police department.  My son originally hired Mr. Ware for his divorce and ended up hiring him for his domestic violence case as well that he got arrested for.   I don't know how he stays in business and still has his license to practice law.  Every time there was a hearing set,  my son had to refresh him and his staff of everything that had gone on since day one, over and over! There was a Google file shared with the attorney,  his staff,  myself and my son.... every time there was new evidence or screen shots, text messages or documentation, they were shared in this file,  which him and his staff had to be reminded of when my son spoke to anyone at his office.
My son and I were told Mr. Ware was a good attorney and he was the best divorce attorney around,  sadly that was not the case with my son's divorce.  My opinion has nothing to do with the final judgement because Mr Ware quit less then 4 days before my sons divorce hearing.
I would not recommend this attorney or his firm! I don't know what type of attorney he was in the past but we were treated disrespectfully and rudely,  we would have fired him but he'd been given alot of money and my son just wanted the divorce finalized.

I recently had the pleasure of working with the Law Office of Travis Ware, and I couldn't be more impressed with their level of professionalism and dedication. From the moment I walked in, I felt welcomed and at ease. Mr. Ware and his team were incredibly knowledgeable and always took the time to explain every detail of my case, and let me know I was in very capable hands. Their commitment to achieving the best possible outcome for me was evident throughout the entire process and when it came to our court date they went in Guns a blazing! I highly recommend the Law Office of Travis Ware to anyone in need of assistance. They truly go above and beyond for their clients!
